raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
February 2026 actuals of $117,100 exceeded the $93,000 budget by $24,100 (+25.9%), driven primarily by a CMO-approved pull-forward of the Q2 digital advertising campaign into February, which consumed what was planned as March and April spend. A Q1 Sales Summit postponement to April partially offset the overrun, reducing Events & Conferences spend by $5,500; a $5,500 venue credit is held and expected to apply against the April event. The forecast for March digital spend is expected to be nil, which should partially self-correct the year-to-date position.

LINE COMMENTARY

Digital Advertising | +$28,500 (+57.0%) | Budget: $50,000 | Actual: $78,500
The Q2 spring campaign was pulled forward from its original March launch into February, with a CMO-approved brief-to-launch window of 28 January to 5 February; this consolidated what was planned as March and April digital budget into a single February invoice of $78,500. The month-end digital report confirms ROAS of 3.2x and CMO sign-off, with March digital spend expected to be nil — though the net full-quarter impact depends on whether April budgets are formally zeroed in the revised forecast, which is noted as pending CFO action.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-19T12:15:00Z - slack-digital_advertising-broad - "Confirmed — moving the Q2 spring campaign to February launch. Agency recommends 28 Jan brief to 5 Feb launch window. This pulls the full March digital budget into February. CMO approved this morning."
- Gmail - 2024-11-19T18:15:00Z - gmail-digital_advertising-broad - "CMO email approving campaign pull-forward. Full Q2 digital budget ($78,500) allocated to February. March and April digital budgets to be zeroed in revised forecast. CFO noted for reforecast."
- Gmail - 2024-11-19T18:45:00Z - gmail-digital_advertising-narrow - "February digital advertising report. Total spend $78,500. ROAS 3.2x. CMO signed off on month-end. March digital line will be nil."

Events & Conferences | -$5,500 (-27.5%) | Budget: $20,000 | Actual: $14,500
The Q1 Sales Summit was postponed to 18 April due to venue availability, with the February slot cancelled on 15 January; a $5,500 venue credit was secured in lieu of a cash refund, and summit costs are expected to land in April. February actuals of $14,500 reflect only a virtual webinar ($1,200) and two meetup sponsorships ($2,800), against which the summit's $5,500 was not incurred.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-20T12:15:00Z - slack-events_&_conferences-broad - "UPDATE on Q1 Sales Summit — moving to April due to venue availability issues. February slot cancelled. Budget savings of $5,500 will carry forward to April event. No refunds needed — venue credit held."
- Gmail - 2024-11-20T18:15:00Z - gmail-events_&_conferences-broad - "Events team email confirming Q1 Sales Summit moved to April. Venue credit of $5,500 secured. No cash outflow in February for this item. Savings tracked against April budget."
- Calendar - 2024-11-20T22:15:00Z - calendar-events_&_conferences-broad - "Q1 Sales Summit rescheduled — now 18 April. Previous February booking cancelled 15 January."
- Gmail - 2024-11-20T18:45:00Z - gmail-events_&_conferences-narrow - "February events invoice pack — virtual webinar $1,200 (Zoom Events), two meetup sponsorships $2,800 (TechMeet SF + ProductCraft NYC). Total $4,000. Summit costs will hit in April."

Content & Creative | +$1,200 (+15.0%) | Budget: $8,000 | Actual: $9,200
New agency Vivid Studio billed the February retainer of $8,000 alongside a $1,200 January strategy workshop that was delivered on 28 January and invoiced in February; the combined invoice of $9,200 was CMO-approved. The overage is a one-period timing item tied to the agency onboarding and is not expected to recur.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-18T12:15:00Z - slack-content_&_creative-broad - "New creative agency (Vivid Studio) briefed in January. Campaign assets delivered ahead of schedule — February billing will include January strategy work ($1,200 extra). Approved by CMO."
- Gmail - 2024-11-18T18:15:00Z - gmail-content_&_creative-broad - "Invoice from Vivid Studio — February retainer $8,000 + January strategy workshop $1,200 = $9,200 total. CMO approved. Strategy work delivered 28 January."

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
The following lines are within acceptable tolerance and require no further investigation at this time.
- Marketing Software: +$100 (+2.0%) — immaterial, no further review required.
- PR & Comms: -$200 (-2.0%) — immaterial, no further review required.
